details.description: Production LibreChat after the Office file-identity release and before generated-artifact visibility filtering

checks: node --check on BaseClient.js, GeneratedArtifactVisibility.js, code-process.js, and mongo-config.js
10,000-artifact classifier benchmark selected one deliverable in 24.532 ms

change_summary.expected_result: A normal generated-file reply shows one complete requested deliverable, explicit reasonable multi-format replies show at most three files, and internal QA or per-page artifacts remain persisted but do not appear as customer download cards.
functions: Classify CodeAPI outputs as deliverable or intermediate without deleting persisted files
Select requested output formats and default to one visible deliverable
Enforce a hard upper bound of three visible generated files per assistant reply
Hide ZIP fallback, per-slide PPTX, rendered pages, manifests, QA, errors, previews, and temporary artifacts
Append the same one-default/three-maximum/no-ZIP contract to GPT and Fable model prompts
limitations: The visibility classifier uses filenames, paths, persisted artifact roles, and the current user request; intentionally unusual filenames may require a later classifier rule
Hidden internal files remain available to the same owner and conversation for continued editing and audit
The separate latest-branch refresh issue is outside this release
change_summary.reason: Conversation a0dc9c6f-ce21-4fae-ab0d-0e8ab25bb108 produced one valid 17-slide PPTX but exposed 51 attachments because LibreChat treated all CodeAPI artifacts as customer deliverables.
change_summary.risk: Enhanced model-tool and Mongo configuration change. A classifier regression could hide a requested output or expose an internal artifact. Deployment uses immutable bind mounts, a Compose backup, a Mongo base-config backup, automatic rollback, and recreates only LibreChat-API.

details.command: Restore the timestamp-matched /opt/librechat/compose.override.yaml.bak-* and generated-artifact-delivery-* Mongo backup created by remote-apply.sh, recreate only LibreChat-API, then verify /, /api/config, /office/, CodeAPI health, normal chat, Office reading, and generated download cards.

validation_plan.expected: The recorded 17-slide incident shape selects only the complete PPTX; default one-file, explicit multi-format, hard cap three, no-ZIP, metadata, BaseClient, and prompt-contract assertions pass.

validation_plan.command: node deployment/production-patches/2026-07-20-office-file-identity/scripts/test-office-file-identity.js plus syntax and governance validation
validation_plan.expected: The prior Office file identity chain and all release-governance checks remain passing.

validation_plan.command: Authenticated vip998 single-turn generated-file acceptance with at most one billable model request
validation_plan.expected: One complete requested file is downloadable, internal artifacts are absent from the assistant message, Mongo retains their owner/conversation metadata, and CodeAPI remains healthy and unchanged.
